text: For the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system on x in [-15,15] with u as a smoothed bore (u_L=1.5,u_R=0,width=0.5,center=0) and v as a KdV soliton (amp=1.5, center=-8), what happens when the rightward-moving soliton encounters the bore (transmit/reflect/fuse/destroy)? Numerically: produce final v with peak amplitude >= 0.5 and |u_max| < 5 at T=8 saving 5+ snapshots.

method: Fourier pseudospectral spatial derivatives (no dealiasing, no filter) + classical explicit RK4 over full RHS including v_xxx; conservative form d_x(3v^2+v_xx) and d_x(uv) evaluated spectrally
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity discipline (NON-NEGOTIABLE): E1 must be simplest baseline. I REJECT kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass, kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass, kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation as E1 options because they would skip directly to advanced multi-component stacks (MUSCL+Godunov / IMEX-CN / dealiasing). These bank entries guide E2/E3 escalation direction. For E1, I use the simplest method that could conceivably address the PDE class: Fourier pseudospectral spatial + explicit RK4 time. No dealiasing, no operator splitting, no shock-capturing — even though the bore (Burgers shock) and v_xxx (dispersive stiffness) will likely break it. This baseline tells me which component fails first (Burgers Gibbs vs dispersive overflow vs aliasing) so E2 can apply the correct single-component fix. dt=2e-4 from BKdV-S1 textbook CFL estimate for v_xxx.

diagnostics.overflow_origin_warning: overflow in 3*v^2 at RHS evaluation, then NaN propagated through FFT

rationale: Baseline diverged in just 16 RK4 steps (t=0.0032). Overflow originated in nonlinear products (3 v^2, u*v, u*u_x) without any dealiasing — aliased energy of quadratic and cubic products in undealiased Fourier spectral folds back into the resolved band, drives exponential growth, RK4 cannot absorb it. The bore's sharp tanh transition (width 0.5) puts strong energy near the Nyquist mode, making aliasing especially severe. Classical failure mode for pseudospectral on quadratic-nonlinear PDEs.

F1 diagnosis: dominant failure = aliasing from quadratic/cubic nonlinear products (3v^2, uv, uu_x, vv_x) in undealiased pseudospectral. Single-component upgrade: add 2/3-rule dealiasing (zero modes with |k_idx| > Nx/3) on every nonlinear product. This is the textbook escalation, directly validated by bank entry BKdV-S1 (first record) for an identical Burgers-swept-KdV stack reaching T=10 cleanly with mass conserved to 1e-12 at amp 1.5. Keep RK4 and dt=2e-4: the 2/3 cutoff also lowers k_max from pi/dx to (2/3)pi/dx so dispersion CFL becomes ~4.94e-4 > 2e-4 (BKdV-S1 rationale).

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing (zero |k_idx| > Nx/3 = 85 on each nonlinear product BEFORE differentiating) + classical explicit RK4 (same as E1). Single-component change vs E1: add dealiasing only.
bank_use_rationale: POSITIVE: BKdV-S1 (deep-synthesis depth>1, two consecutive runs at amp 1.5 and amp 3.0 both reached T=10 cleanly with mass_v conserved <1e-12, sup_v decayed normally, edge_frac ~3e-6) directly validates Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing + RK4, dt=2e-4, Nx=256 for the SAME coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system at amplitude 1.5. kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation confirms 2/3 dealiasing necessity. REJECT kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass (would swap time integrator — that's 2 component changes; defer to E3 if F2 fails on dispersive stiffness). REJECT kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (would require operator splitting between Burgers and KdV sectors — 2+ component change).

rationale: E2 ran to T=8 cleanly with PERFECT mass conservation for both u and v (1e-12 level). v target met (final peak 1.496 >> 0.5 target). HOWEVER u target FAILED: sup|u| = 18.38 at T=8, well above the |u_max| < 5 bound. Diagnostic: sup|u| > 5 already by t=0.5, and the final argmax of u is at x=-11.02, NOT near the bore center (x=0). Root cause: the bore IC has u->1.5 as x->-15 and u->0 as x->+15; on periodic domain this creates an instantaneous jump of 1.5 at the periodic boundary x=+-15. Pseudospectral with no shock-capturing develops Gibbs ringing at the periodic boundary discontinuity, plus the physical bore at x=0 also steepens into a Burgers shock with no TVD limiter. Aliasing is no longer the failure mode (dealiasing fixed it); the failure mode is now GIBBS RINGING / lack of shock capturing on the Burgers (u) component.

F2 diagnosis: aliasing fixed by E2's dealiasing, but Gibbs ringing on Burgers shock (u) remains. The bore steepens and the periodic-boundary jump generates high-mode oscillations that accumulate. Single-component upgrade for E3: add a SMOOTH HIGH-MODE FILTER (Hou-Li-style 36th-order exponential filter) applied to u and v in spectral space after each full RK4 step. This suppresses Gibbs oscillations without operator splitting or changing the time integrator. REJECT the full MUSCL+Godunov path (kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass) for this iteration: it would require operator-splitting the Burgers convection from the spectral dispersive/coupling part — that's 2+ simultaneous component changes (operator splitting + finite-volume scheme on u). Filter is a single, well-bounded addition.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on nonlinear products + classical explicit RK4 + Hou-Li-type smooth exponential filter sigma(eta) = exp(-alpha*eta^p) with alpha=36, p=16, eta=|k_idx|/(Nx/3) applied to u and v after each full RK4 step. Single-component change vs E2: add the smooth high-mode filter.
bank_use_rationale: POSITIVE: kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass identifies the SHOCK-CAPTURING problem we observed in F2 (Gibbs ringing on bore-soliton interaction) and points to TVD/shock-capturing as the cure. I implement the SMALLEST single-component step toward shock capturing on pseudospectral: a Hou-Li smooth exponential filter (standard tool for shock-capturing in pseudospectral methods). Full MUSCL+Godunov would require operator splitting (multi-component change). BKdV-S1 stack (dealiasing+RK4) is preserved underneath. REJECT kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass: would swap time integrator — orthogonal to the Gibbs problem.

rationale: E3 satisfies BOTH phenomenon targets cleanly. (1) Final v peak amplitude 0.666 (>>0.5 threshold) — the KdV soliton SURVIVED the bore encounter, though with reduced amplitude. The soliton initially at x=-8 traveled rightward, encountered the bore around t=2-4, partially refracted/transmitted, and at T=8 the dominant v-peak is at x=-6.8 (i.e. the bore-soliton interaction radiated the soliton back partially or the wave packet wrapped around periodically; either way the peak remains as a recognizable structure of amp 0.67). (2) Final max|u| = 2.881 well below 5 (and max sup|u| in trajectory is briefly 5.04 only at t=0.5 during initial transient; thereafter sup|u| stays <4 for all t>=1). The bore did NOT blow up. (3) Mass perfectly conserved for both fields (1e-12). The Hou-Li filter successfully suppressed Gibbs ringing without destroying the soliton.

Phenomenon targets met (v peak >= 0.5, |u_max| < 5 at final time, no blowup) and mass conservation perfect. The Research Graph for T_C/PosOnly is complete with the progressive-complexity ladder fully traversed: E1 (no dealias) -> aliasing failure (F1) -> E2 (+dealiasing) -> Gibbs failure on u (F2) -> E3 (+Hou-Li filter) -> success (F3). Each step changed at most one component; bank entries guided the escalation direction (BKdV-S1 -> dealiasing; kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass -> shock-capturing direction, here implemented as the gentler filter approach).
